info.name: NBES — RBAC Admin (Sprint 1.2)
info.description:
End-to-end test suite for the NBES Roles, Permissions & RBAC Gateway.

## Setup

1. Start NBES locally: `./venv/bin/python manage.py migrate && ./venv/bin/python manage.py runserver 8003`.
2. Generate a dev admin token (HS256, signed with `JWT_SECRET_KEY` from `.env`). Paste the output into the `admin_token` collection variable.
   ```bash
   ./venv/bin/python -c "
import jwt, datetime, uuid
import os, django
os.environ.setdefault('DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE', 'config.settings.dev')
django.setup()
from django.conf import settings
print(jwt.encode({
    'sub': str(uuid.uuid4()),
    'email': 'admin@nbes.test',
    'realm_access': {'roles': ['system-administrator']},
    'exp': int((datetime.datetime.utcnow() + datetime.timedelta(hours=24)).timestamp()),
}, settings.JWT_SECRET_KEY, algorithm='HS256'))"
   ```
3. Generate a non-admin token the same way, swap the role to `item-writer`. Paste into `writer_token`.
4. Run the requests in order. `Create role` writes `role_id` into the collection; subsequent requests reuse it.

## Notes

- Production uses RS256 Keycloak tokens. This collection targets the dev shared-secret path. The endpoints are identical.
- Every mutation emits an `AuditEvent` row. Every 403 emits `AUTHZ_DENIED`. Inspect via Django admin or `apps/audit`.

request.description:
Returns what NBES thinks the bearer can do. `roles_in_jwt` is straight from the token; `roles_recognised_by_nbes` is the subset that maps to an active `Role` row; `permissions` is the resolved set the gateway enforces.

Use this to confirm the admin token works before running any mutation.
Lists every codename NBES enforces. Seeded from `apps/users/migrations/0001_initial.py`. Codenames are declared in code (`HasPermission(...)`) and cannot be added via the API — that's by design.

response.name: 200 OK

request.description: Lists every role NBES recognises (mirror of IAM role names NBES has been told about). Includes the codenames granted to each.

response.name: 200 OK

exec: // Save the new role id for later requests in this run.

request.description:
Tells NBES it should recognise a role name. This does NOT create the role in Keycloak — IAM does that. NBES will only resolve permissions for roles registered here.

Idempotent: re-posting the same name returns the existing row with `200 OK`.

response.name: 201 Created — new role
200 OK — already exists
200 OK
200 OK

request.description: Marks the role inactive. Historical audit rows still resolve. Users holding this role lose every NBES permission within 60 s (cache invalidation is immediate on this node).

response.name: 200 OK

request.description: Replaces the grant set with the supplied list. The view diffs against the current grants and reports `added` / `removed`. Cache is invalidated immediately for this role.

response.name: 400 — unknown codename

request.description: Empty list ⇒ every grant on this role is removed. Useful for snapshot / restore tests.

item.name: 4 · Denial scenarios
item.description: Confirms the gateway rejects unauthorised callers AND records the audit event. Use the `writer_token` variable (a JWT carrying only `item-writer` in `realm_access.roles`).

request.description: An item-writer should never see the admin surface. Expect 403 AUTHZ_DENIED and an `AuditEvent(action="AUTHZ_DENIED")` row.

response.name: 403 Forbidden

request.description: `/me/permissions` is open to any authenticated user. The writer sees only `item:create`.

item.name: Unknown role in JWT (expect zero permissions)

request.description: Mint a token whose `realm_access.roles` is something NBES has never been told about (e.g. `nbes-ghost`). The endpoint returns 200 with an empty `permissions` set — the local Role registry filters it out. This is the cross-system safety property: an Examiner-in-AMS cannot impersonate an Examiner-in-NBES.
